task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the Drupal Commerce Orders list or an Order detail page showing John Doe's order (totaling $1,298.00) with its status changed to 'Canceled'.

task_completion.description: Create a promotion named 'Store Credit - John Doe Refund'
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the Promotions admin page or a promotion configuration screen showing a promotion titled exactly 'Store Credit - John Doe Refund'.

task_completion.description: Ensure the promotion is active and store-assigned
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a checked 'Active' checkbox and the 'Stores' field selecting 'Urban Electronics' on the promotion configuration page.

task_completion.description: Set the promotion offer to a fixed dollar amount of $1,298.00
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the 'Offer' section on the promotion page showing the type as a fixed amount off the order total, with the amount explicitly set to '1298.00'.

task_completion.description: Require a coupon with the code 'REFUND-JOHNDOE'
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the 'Coupons' section within the promotion configuration or the coupon list showing a generated coupon with the exact code 'REFUND-JOHNDOE'.

task_completion.description: Set the coupon usage limit to 1
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the coupon configuration or details showing the 'Usage limit' or 'Maximum usage' field set to '1'.

integrity.description: The agent used the web browser to interact with the Drupal Commerce GUI.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show a web browser navigating the Urban Electronics admin panel, not terminal commands (like drush) or database queries executing the changes.

integrity.description: The agent actually configured the promotion and order in the system.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show form fields being filled out, dropdowns being selected, and save buttons being clicked in the Drupal interface, rather than just a text document with the required values.

integrity.description: The agent did not use browser developer tools to spoof the success state.
integrity.visual_evidence: There should be no browser developer tools (Elements/Console tab) open modifying the DOM to fake the order status or promotion details.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Customer: John Doe (username: johndoe, uid: 2). Order products: Apple MacBook Air M2 13-inch (APPLE-MBA-M2-13) and Keychron Q1 Pro Mechanical Keyboard (KEYCHRON-Q1PRO). Expected order total and refund amount: $1298.00. Expected coupon code: REFUND-JOHNDOE.
